name: Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38)
name_original: Ruta de los Tres Castillos – circular desde Guadamur (PR-TO 38)
description: Hike the moderate 8.6-mile Route of the Three Castles from Guadamur, exploring historical castles, olive groves, and local wildlife.
region_intro: Hiking around Guadamur offers diverse landscapes within the Montes de Toledo region. The area is characterized by extensive olive groves and natural settings conducive to outdoor exploration. Hikers can observe various birds of prey, including imperial eagles and griffon vultures, in their natural habitat. The terrain features rolling hills and historical landmarks, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. Best hiking trails around Guadamur The most popular hiking route is Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38), an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route allows hikers to traverse historical castles, olive groves, and observe local wildlife. Another top favourite among local hikers is Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Guajaraz reservoir and surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Pico de Noez — round trip from Noez, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Guadamur is defined by olive groves, historical castles, and the natural environment of the Montes de Toledo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to moderate trails. The routes in Guadamur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 2100 hikers have used komoot to explore Guadamur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Guadamur?
A: Hiking around Guadamur offers diverse landscapes within the Montes de Toledo region. You'll find extensive olive groves, rolling hills, and natural settings that are home to various birds of prey, including imperial eagles and griffon vultures. The area also features historical landmarks integrated into the natural environment.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Guadamur?
A: Yes, Guadamur offers a good selection of trails for various ability levels. Out of the 78 hiking routes available, 37 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk.

Q: What historical attractions can I explore on a hike in Guadamur?
A: Guadamur is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019433717/route-of-the-three-castles-pr-to-38" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable trail that allows you to traverse historical castles and olive groves. The town itself boasts a well-preserved 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadamur Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and other historical monuments like the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Natividad and the Parish Church of Santa María Magdalena are also points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Guadamur area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018261137/embalse-de-guajaraz-circular-from-arges" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.7 km path offering views of the Guajaraz reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018786963/pico-de-noez-circular-via-the-trenches-trail-pr-to-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez – circular via the Trenches Trail (PR-TO 23)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.8 km route.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Guadamur?
A: The hiking routes in Guadamur are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 260 reviews. Hikers often praise the blend of natural beauty, historical landmarks, and the peaceful environment of the olive groves and Montes de Toledo.

Q: Can I see any wildlife, especially birds, while hiking in Guadamur?
A: Yes, the natural environment around Guadamur is excellent for wildlife observation, particularly birds of prey. Hikers often have the opportunity to spot imperial eagles, griffon vultures, and black vultures soaring above the Montes de Toledo.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018261137/embalse-de-guajaraz-circular-from-arges" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Guajaraz — circular from Argés&lt;/a&gt; route offers scenic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guajaraz Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the surrounding countryside.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots in the Guadamur hiking area?
A: While hiking, you can find several viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guajaraz Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic views. Additionally, nearby highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Barrancas de Burujón and the Castrejón Reservoir from Mirador del Cambrón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do hikes in Guadamur typically take?
A: Hike durations in Guadamur vary depending on the route and your pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019433717/route-of-the-three-castles-pr-to-38" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38)&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 34 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197046568/pico-de-noez-round-trip-from-noez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Noez — round trip from Noez&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2 hours and 15 minutes.

Q: Are there any hiking opportunities near Guadamur that extend towards Toledo?
A: Yes, Guadamur's proximity to Toledo means you can also explore hiking opportunities in the broader Toledo area. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toledo Alcázar – Toledo Cathedral loop from Old Synagogue Santa Maria la Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a different historical and urban hiking experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Guadamur?
A: The Montes de Toledo region, where Guadamur is located, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making it comfortable to explore the olive groves and historical sites. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cooler, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for spotting local flora and fauna?
A: Many trails, especially those winding through the olive groves and the natural areas of the Montes de Toledo, offer opportunities to observe local flora and fauna. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019433717/route-of-the-three-castles-pr-to-38" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Castles (PR-TO 38)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically mentioned for its wildlife observation potential.
tips: Its history is a mystery, but news arrives from the year 1116, during a battle between the Christians of Toledo and the Muslims of Oreja. Warden Farax Abdalí was defeated near the Christian castle of Polán, which was perhaps under construction at the time.

This fortress is a magnificent, well-preserved example of the fifteenth-century residential castles found in the province of Toledo. Currently inhabited, we can tour its exterior, appreciating the aesthetic result of the influences of Italian art in its architecture.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Route of the Three Castles - circular from Guadamur (PR-TO 38)?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this 13.8 km circular route is around 3 hours and 34 minutes. This duration is for hiking and does not include stops for sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Route of the Three Castles?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily winds through agricultural lands, including olive groves and livestock farms, traversing the municipalities of Guadamur and Polán. You'll encounter a variety of paths, including local roads, canal paths, and tracks. Specifically, it features approximately 9.84 km of paths/trails ("senda"), 2.93 km of streets ("calle"), 657 m of dirt tracks/paths ("sendero"), and sections of paved and unpaved tracks.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks will I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history. You'll pass by the archaeological site of Guarrazar (6th century), the impressive 15th-century Guadamur Castle, the towers of the ancient Polán fortress (12th century), and Torre Cervatos (12th century). The trail also goes through the "paraje de los Moros," a site of historical significance.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Three Castles dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but as it passes through agricultural lands and near livestock farms, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and property.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts from Guadamur. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Guadamur near the trailhead, as it's a popular starting point for local routes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot in the Toledo province, and winters can be cooler, so dress accordingly.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, and there are no entrance fees to hike the Route of the Three Castles - circular from Guadamur (PR-TO 38). It is a public access trail.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Keep an eye out for local wildlife such as red partridges, hares, and rabbits. The area is also home to various raptors, including the imperial eagle, griffon vulture, and black vulture.

faqs.question: Are there any safety considerations or specific warnings for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you should exercise caution when crossing the CM-401 highway at two points along the route. While the trail signaling for hikers is good, vehicles may not always be aware of hikers crossing.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with sections of several other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Ruta de los tres Castillos, Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 1, Camino de Polán a Guadamur, and Camino a Toledo.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Guadamur, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities within the town where you can refresh before or after your hike.
